title: cinchona micrantha var. oblongifolia
intro: Cinchona micrantha var. oblongifolia is a plant species within the Rubiaceae family, closely related to the cinchona tree, known for its medicinal properties. It contains alkaloids, particularly quinine, which have been used to treat malaria and other febrile diseases. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Cinchona micrantha var. oblongifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
